title: Argentinian Pilaf Bowl
description: A flavorful and nutritious meal inspired by Argentine cuisine, featuring a hearty pilaf with a hint of spice.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the diced bell pepper and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked black beans, cumin, and pilaf spice blend. Stir to combine.
Add the rice to the saucepan and stir to combine with the vegetable mixture.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the rice is lightly toasted.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 18-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
